answers.answer: A) Generate a new key - update the service to accept both old and new keys - deploy - switch clients to the new key - revoke the old key. This is the standard zero-downtime key rotation pattern: (1) Generate new key; (2) Update service to accept BOTH keys (dual validation); (3) Deploy; (4) Switch clients to new key; (5) Revoke old key. Ensures no downtime and allows rollback. Options B/C revoke before updating causing outage. Option D schedules unnecessary downtime.

UPGRADE PLAN (sequential order):

1. TypeScript 4.5->5.3 (2-3 days): npm install typescript@5.3. Fix stricter type errors. Independent, do first.

2. React 17->18 (2-3 days): npm install react@18 react-dom@18. Replace ReactDOM.render with createRoot. Test concurrent rendering.

3. react-router v5->v6 (5-7 days): npm install react-router-dom@6. npx react-router-codemod v5-to-v6. Switch Switch->Routes, useHistory->useNavigate, component->element prop. 50 routes need updating.

4. Material UI v4->v5 (7-10 days): npm install @mui/material@5 @emotion/react @emotion/styled. npx @mui/codemod v5.0.0/preset-safe. JSS->emotion, 200 components + design system migration.

5. webpack 4->5 (2-3 days): npm install webpack@5. Update config for asset modules, add Node.js polyfills if needed.

Total: 18-26 days (1 dev) or 10-13 days (2 devs). Risk mitigation: feature branches, codemods first, staging test, canary deploy, rollback plan per step.
